{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 采女氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 采女[[臣]]
のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[穂積氏]] |氏祖 = [[采女宮手]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「采女」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。姓は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。[[穂積真津]]の次男・[[采女宮手]]を祖とする、[[穂積氏]]から分かれた氏族である。 __TOC__
==概要== 穂積氏祖の穂積真津の次男・宮手が采女臣の姓を賜ったのが始まりとされる。[[新撰姓氏録]]では、「右京神別 天神 采女朝臣 - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後」と記されている。 ==外部リンク== * [http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02105.htm 日本の苗字7000傑 姓氏類別大観　穂積氏] {{DEFAULTSORT:うねめうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:氏]]

のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[穂積氏]] |氏祖 = [[采女宮手]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = [[采女枚夫]] |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「[[采女]]」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。[[穂積真津]]の次男・[[采女宮手]]を祖とする、[[穂積氏]]から分かれた氏族である。 __TOC__
==概要== 穂積氏祖の穂積真津の次男・宮手が采女臣の姓を賜ったのが始まりとされる。[[新撰姓氏録]]では、「右京神別 天神 采女朝臣 - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後」と記されている。[[采女]]の統括を担当した[[伴造]]氏族。もと臣姓であったが、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に朝臣姓に改姓している。後に[[采女枚夫]]が[[正五位下]]に昇った。 ==外部リンク== * [http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02105.htm 日本の苗字7000傑 姓氏類別大観　穂積氏] {{DEFAULTSORT:うねめうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:氏]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 采女氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 采女[[臣]]
のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[穂積氏]] |氏祖 = [[采女宮手]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = [[采女枚夫]] |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「[[采女]]」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。[[穂積真津]]の次男・[[采女宮手]]を祖とする、[[穂積氏]]から分かれた氏族である。 __TOC__
==概要== 穂積氏祖の穂積真津の次男・宮手が采女臣の姓を賜ったのが始まりとされる。[[新撰姓氏録]]では、「右京神別 天神 采女朝臣 - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後」と記されている。[[采女]]の統括を担当した[[伴造]]氏族。もと臣姓であったが、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に朝臣姓に改姓している。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[采女摩礼志]]は、[[中臣御食子]]と[[高向宇摩]]とともに[[推古天皇]]崩御後の次期天皇に[[田村皇子]]を推した。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[官吏]]である[[采女竹羅]]は、[[天武天皇]]10年に[[小錦下]]・遣新羅大使となった。同13年には[[三野王]]と新都の地を探すため[[信濃国]]に派遣された。同年朝臣(あそん)の姓(かばね)をあたえられる。[[持統天皇]]3年頃死去。名は筑羅、竹良とも。持統天皇3年12月25日に建立された采女氏塋域碑には「飛鳥浄原大朝庭大弁官直大弐采女竹良卿」と記されていたとされる。 後に[[采女枚夫]]が[[正五位下]]に昇った。 ==外部リンク== * [http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02105.htm 日本の苗字7000傑 姓氏類別大観　穂積氏] {{DEFAULTSORT:うねめうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:氏]]

のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[穂積氏]] |氏祖 = [[采女宮手]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = [[采女枚夫]] |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「[[采女]]」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。[[穂積真津]]の次男・[[采女宮手]]を祖とする、[[穂積氏]]から分かれた氏族である。 __TOC__

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 采女氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 采女[[臣]]
のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[穂積氏]] |氏祖 = [[采女宮手]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = [[采女枚夫]] |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「[[采女]]」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。 [[穂積真津]]の次男・[[采女宮手]]を祖とする、[[穂積氏]]から分かれた氏族である。[[采女]]の統括を担当した[[伴造]]氏族。 __TOC__
==概要== 穂積氏祖の穂積真津の次男・宮手が[[尾張国]]丹羽評に移住し采女臣の姓を賜ったのが始まりとされる。[[新撰姓氏録]]では、「右京神別 天神 采女朝臣 - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後」と記されている。もと臣姓であったが、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に朝臣姓に改姓している。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[采女摩礼志]]は、[[中臣御食子]]と[[高向宇摩]]とともに[[推古天皇]]崩御後の次期天皇に[[田村皇子]]を推した。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[官吏]]である[[采女竹羅]]は、[[天武天皇]]10年に[[小錦下]]・遣新羅大使となった。同13年には[[三野王]]と新都の地を探すため[[信濃国]]に派遣された。[[八色の姓]]制定では朝臣の姓を賜っている。[[持統天皇]]3年頃死去。名は筑羅、竹良とも。持統天皇3年12月25日に建立された采女氏塋域碑には「飛鳥浄原大朝庭[[大弁官]][[直大弐]]采女竹良卿」と記されていたとされる。 後に[[采女枚夫]]が[[正五位下]]に昇った。 ==外部リンク== * [http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02105.htm 日本の苗字7000傑 姓氏類別大観　穂積氏] {{DEFAULTSORT:うねめうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:氏]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 采女氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 采女[[臣]]
のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[物部氏]] |氏祖 = [[大水口宿禰命]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = [[采女竹羅]]
[[采女枚夫]] |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「[[采女]]」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。[[采女]]の統括を担当した[[伴造]]氏族。 __TOC__ {{clear}} == 概要 == 穂積氏祖の穂積真津の次男・宮手が[[尾張国]][[丹羽評]]に移住し采女臣の姓を賜ったのが始まりとされる。[[新撰姓氏録]]では、「右京神別 天神 采女朝臣 - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後」と記されている。もと臣姓であったが、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に朝臣姓に改姓している。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[采女摩礼志]]は、[[中臣御食子]]と[[高向宇摩]]とともに[[推古天皇]]崩御後の次期天皇に[[舒明天皇|田村皇子]]を推した。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[官吏]]である[[采女竹羅]]は、[[天武天皇]]10年に[[小錦下]]・遣新羅大使となった。同13年には[[三野王]]と新都の地を探すため[[信濃国]]に派遣された。[[八色の姓]]制定では朝臣の姓を賜っている。[[持統天皇]]3年頃死去。名は筑羅、竹良とも。持統天皇3年12月25日に建立された采女氏塋域碑には「飛鳥浄原大朝庭[[大弁官]][[直大弐]]采女竹良卿」と記されていたとされる。 後に[[采女枚夫]]が[[正五位下]]に昇った。 == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20021224155535/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02105.htm 日本の苗字7000傑 姓氏類別大観　穂積氏] {{DEFAULTSORT:うねめうし}} [[Category:采女氏|*]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]]

{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:kk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted [[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." revision.comment: Adding [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:pages revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:pages An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Saiyō {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Saiyō Clan-qq]] [[Category:Saiyō Clan]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] to [[Category:q-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}-q]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." [[ja:v]]

{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion [[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion [[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion [[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Saiyō Clan-qq]] [[Category:Saiyō Clan]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion [[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Saiyō Clan]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion
