{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Q--q]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Saiyō Clan]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}[[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天武天皇}}" to "|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}}"
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "天武天皇" to "Emperor Tenmu"
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|新撰姓氏録" to "|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku"
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|八色の姓}}" to "|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}"
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier 3]] {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier {{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} [[Category:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:completion tier revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}

Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|12=draft|12=draft}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|12=draft|12=draft}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}}

revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:采女氏 up to rev 100476546
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 采女氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 采女[[臣]]
のち采女[[朝臣]] |始祖 = [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]] |出自 = [[物部氏]] |氏祖 = [[大水口宿禰命]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = [[采女竹羅]]
[[采女枚夫]] |後裔 = }} '''采女氏'''（うねめうじ）は、「[[采女]]」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]は始め'''采女臣'''、後に'''采女朝臣'''。[[采女]]の統括を担当した[[伴造]]氏族。 __TOC__ {{clear}} == 概要 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、「右京神別 天神 采女朝臣 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の[[大水口宿禰命|大水口宿禰]]の後」と記されている。もとは臣姓であったが、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に朝臣姓に改姓している。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[采女摩礼志]]は、[[中臣御食子]]と[[高向宇摩]]とともに[[推古天皇]]崩御後の次期天皇に[[舒明天皇|田村皇子]]を推した。 [[飛鳥時代]]の[[官吏]]である[[采女竹羅]]は、[[天武天皇]]10年に[[小錦下]]・遣新羅大使となった。同13年には[[三野王]]と新都の地を探すため[[信濃国]]に派遣された。[[八色の姓]]制定では朝臣の姓を賜っている。[[持統天皇]]3年頃死去。名は筑羅、竹良とも。持統天皇3年12月25日に建立された采女氏塋域碑には「飛鳥浄原大朝庭[[大弁官]][[直大弐]]采女竹良卿」と記されていたとされる。 後に[[采女枚夫]]が[[正五位下]]に昇った。 == 外部リンク == {{DEFAULTSORT:うねめうし}} [[Category:采女氏|*]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:神別]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[采女氏]] to [[Saiyō Clan]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Category:Japanese clans]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{DEFAULTSORT:Uneme Clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saiyō Clan}} {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]]

Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=}} | Origin = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomo no Miyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor {{ill|Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomei|qq=}}) as the successor to {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Divine Lineage]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=}} | Origin = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomo no Miyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor {{ill|Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomei|qq=}}) as the successor to {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]]

[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]

In the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor {{ill|Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomei|qq=}}) as the successor to {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]]
