revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Uneme Clan|*]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=}} | Origin = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomo no Miyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor {{ill|Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomei|qq=}}) as the successor to {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]]

later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}} after {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}}'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=}} | Origin = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor {{ill|Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomei|qq=}}) as the successor to {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}}'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]]

Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] after [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]]

The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:穂積氏
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] after [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]

Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] after [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Owari Province]] [[Category:Clans of Shinano Province]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684 CE). During the [[Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] after [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舒明天皇}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]

In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舒明天皇}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saiyō Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese clans | clan_name = Uneme Clan (采女氏) | image = | surname = Uneme [[臣]]
later Uneme [[朝臣]] | founder = [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] | origin = [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe Clan]] | progenitor = [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] (Heavenly Deities) | ancestral_home = | notable_figures = [[Uneme Takera]]
[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684 CE). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] after [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舒明天皇}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]

In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|Clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舒明天皇}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]

[[Uneme Hiramai]] | descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏) refers to an ancient Japanese clan that bore the name "Uneme" and served as administrators overseeing court attendants ([[Uneme]]). Initially bearing the title '''Uneme no Omī''', the clan later assumed the title '''Uneme no Asomi'''. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records the clan as "Right Capital Divine Lineage, Heavenly Deities, Uneme no Asomi, related to the Ishikami no Asomi. They are descendants of [[Nigihayahi]], with [[Ōminakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] as their ancestor." Originally titled with the ''Omī'' Rank, the clan was elevated to ''Asomi'' in the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (684 CE). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], notable members included [[Uneme Mareji]], who, alongside [[Nakatomi no Mikeko]] and [[Takamukai no Uma]], supported the succession of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] after [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]'s death. Another prominent figure was [[Uneme Takera]], a court official during the same period. In the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign, Takera held the Rank of ''Shōkōnige'' (Small Cap, Lower Rank) and served as the Envoy to Silla. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with Prince {{ill|Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore sites in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for the construction of a new capital. Upon the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Colors of Rank|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Colors of Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, he was granted the ''Asomi'' title. Takera is believed to have died around the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign. An epitaph erected on December 25 of that year referred to him as "Takera of Uneme, Minister of the Great Court at Asuka Kiyohara, Senior Deputy of the {{ill|Ōbenkan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbenkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}}." Later, [[Uneme Hiramai]] achieved the Rank of ''Shōgoige'' (Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade). == External Links == {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[ja:v]] ==Merged second translation== {{Ancient Japanese Clan | Name = Uneme Clan | image = | Title = Uneme [[Omi]]
Later Uneme [[Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} | Origin = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] | Clan founder = {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}} | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | Territory = | Notable persons = {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}
{{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} | Descendants = }} '''Uneme Clan''' (采女氏, ''Uneme Uji'') was an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|Clan]] named after "Uneme" (court ladies). The [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of the clan was originally '''Uneme Omi''' and later changed to '''Uneme Ason'''. The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舒明天皇}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]

The clan was a [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomobe]] responsible for overseeing {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}. __TOC__ {{clear}} == Overview == According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], “Right Capital, {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}: Uneme Ason, co-descendants of Ishikami Ason. They trace their lineage to {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, whose sixth-generation descendant was {{ill|Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizuguchi no Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水口宿禰}}.” Initially, their kabane was ''Omi,'' but it was changed to ''Ason'' during the 13th year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s reign (684) with the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reforms. In the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], {{ill|Uneme Mareishi|ja|采女摩礼志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Mareishi|qq=}} supported the selection of {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舒明天皇}} (later Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Jomei]]) as the successor to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] after her passing, alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Mikeko|ja|中臣御食子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Mikeko|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamukai no Uma|ja|高向宇摩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamukai no Uma|qq=}}. Another prominent figure, {{ill|Uneme Takera|ja|采女竹羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Takera|qq=}}, served as a {{ill|Kōninkan|ja|小錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōninkan|qq=}} and envoy to Silla in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign. In the 13th year, he was dispatched with {{ill|Prince Mino|ja|三野王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Mino|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 美努王}} to explore possible locations for a new capital in Shinano Province. During the Yakusa no Kabane reforms, he was granted the title ''Ason.'' He passed away around the 3rd year of Empress [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]]'s reign. His tombstone, erected on December 25 of the same year, described him as “{{ill|Dainikan|ja|大弁官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} of Asuka Clean Plain Grand Court, Chokudaifu Uneme Takera Lord.” His name also appears as ''Chikura'' or ''Takeyoshi.'' Later, {{ill|Uneme Hikafu|ja|采女枚夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Hikafu|qq=}} rose to the Rank of ''Shōgoi no Ge'' ([[Senior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade). == External Links == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|采女氏|version=100476546|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Uneme clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Uneme clan]] [[ja:采女氏]] [[sn:neme clan]] [[sn:Saiyō Clan]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:尾張国の氏族]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:采女氏]]
